title: Messi scripts never-seen-before WC history as Argentina complete epic comeback
content: Lionel Messi achieved a historic World Cup milestone by scoring in six consecutive knockout matches. Argentina secured a dramatic 3-2 victory against Egypt after trailing by two goals. Messi's equaliser in the 83rd minute ignited a remarkable turnaround for his team. Enzo Fernandez then completed the stunning comeback in stoppage time to advance Argentina.

title: 'He deserved it': Baruipur rape-murder accused's mother refuses to accept body after encounter
content: The mother of the main accused in the Baruipur case refused his body.  Prabash Mondal died in a police encounter while reconstructing the crime scene.  He allegedly tried to snatch a firearm and fired at the police team.  The post-mortem report revealed the 12-year-old victim was sexually assaulted and tortured.  Investigators added charges of gang rape and murder against three other suspects.

title: Meta launches AI image models, company's 'highest-paid employee' shares a photo tip
content: Meta launched Muse Image, its first AI image generator from Meta Superintelligence Labs, alongside a preview of Muse Video. Chief AI officer Alexandr Wang, one of the company's highest-paid hires, urged users to keep it personal: upload your own photos, tag friends, and pull from trends. The launch also drew privacy backlash over a feature that uses public Instagram photos by default.

title: Watch: Starmer, Støre swap playful WC jibes before England-Norway last 8 clash
content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre met at a NATO summit. The leaders exchanged light-hearted banter while wearing their national football team jerseys. They discussed their countries' upcoming FIFA World Cup quarterfinal match. Norway's Prime Minister recalled a past victory against England in qualification. Both leaders anticipate a great and exciting football match.

title: Germany builds alternative to US Patriot system - all about Diehl’s IRIS‑T SLX
content: The IRIS-T air-defense system is undergoing significant enhancements by the German firm Diehl Defence. With a new longer-range variant in the works, this initiative seeks to diminish reliance on often-limited US Patriot missile interceptors. The escalating global appetite for air defense, fueled by recent conflicts, underscores the urgency for the IRIS-T SLX, which promises superior functionality and integration with existing systems, signifying a crucial step towards Europe's defense independence.

title: 'BJP petrol pump': AAP shares 'E20 premium receipt' in dig over ethanol blending
content: The graphic, styled as a fuel station receipt, described the product as "E20 Premium Receipt" and claimed the blend contained "Petrol 80%" and "Ethanol 20%". It also carried lines such as "Customer Choice 0%", "Price Reduction Rs 0.00", "Mileage Impact 20-30%", "Extra Maintenance Possible" and concluded with "TOTAL CHARGED Full petrol price" and "TOTAL VALUE Reduced mileage". Another line read, "Terms and conditions not explained".

title: China may be planning to tell America: We do not need your Nvidia chips
content: Chinese AI firm DeepSeek is developing its own inference chips. This move aims to lessen dependence on Nvidia's hardware. US export controls have already impacted Nvidia's sales in China. Huawei is also a significant domestic competitor in this space. DeepSeek joins other AI developers creating in-house chip solutions.

title: Close call: Air India, AI Express planes come face-to-face at Mumbai airport
content: A potential runway collision was averted at Mumbai airport Tuesday night. An Air India flight aborted its take-off after air traffic control instructions. This occurred while an Air India Express aircraft was still on the same runway. The Air India Express flight had arrived from Siliguri and had not yet vacated. The incident is expected to be examined by aviation authorities.

title: Scientists thickened Arctic ice with seawater, but there's a catch
content: Scientists tested spraying seawater onto Arctic ice, which showed promising initial results. The treated ice became thicker and more reflective, resisting summer melting effectively. This method offers an alternative to controversial geoengineering approaches for ice preservation. While promising, researchers note significant challenges for large-scale implementation across the Arctic. Further work is essential before this technique can be considered for broader application.

title: After thousands of layoffs, Microsoft’s cost cutting drive hits OpenAI and Anthropic
content: Microsoft is replacing external AI models with its own internally developed systems.  This strategic shift aims to significantly reduce operational costs for the tech giant.  Tens of thousands of AI prompts are now handled by Microsoft's own MAI models.  The company is developing competitive AI capabilities at a much lower expense.  This move aligns with a stated goal to decrease financial dependence on outside AI labs.

title: 'Ronaldo's legacy is untouchable': Thierry Henry's heartfelt message
content: Thierry Henry believes Cristiano Ronaldo has nothing left to prove.  His football legacy is considered untouchable and will stand the test of time.  Ronaldo's dedication to fitness and professionalism serves as an example.  He concluded his World Cup career with 27 appearances and 11 goals.  His impact extends beyond trophies and records in the sport.

title: Baruipur horror: Accused in 12-year-old's brutal rape and murder shot dead in police encounter
content: Prabhas Mondal, the main accused in the Baruipur rape and murder case, has been killed.  He was killed in a police encounter while officers reconstructed the crime scene.  Mondal allegedly attempted to snatch a firearm from an officer during the reconstruction.  He then opened fire at the police team, which led to retaliation.  The police fired back, and Mondal was declared dead at the hospital.

title: Another split brewing? Sharad NCP's Jayant Patil meets BJP leader Vinod Tawde
content: A meeting between Jayant Patil and Vinod Tawde sparked speculation about NCP (SP) joining the NDA. Patil described the encounter as nonpolitical, occurring during a parliamentary committee visit. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis denied any attempts to engineer a split within Sharad Pawar's party. NCP (SP) MP Amol Kolhe's earlier statement also fueled these rumors and discussions. Supriya Sule refuted the speculation, stating she was unaware of the meeting.

title: Watch: Tempers explode as Messi clashes with Egypt coach, staff member sent off
content: Lionel Messi and Egypt's coach Hossam Hassan clashed after Argentina's victory. A member of Egypt's staff received a red card for confronting the Argentine captain. This incident overshadowed Argentina's dramatic 3-2 comeback win in the tournament. Egypt's coach expressed strong dissatisfaction with the match officiating. The confrontation sparked debate regarding FIFA's anti-racism protocols.

title: UGC NET June 2026 answer key soon: How to check OMR sheets, objection window
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) is expected to release the UGC NET June 2026 provisional answer key between July 15 and July 20 after the examination concluded on July 5. Around 9.08 lakh candidates will be able to access their recorded OMR sheets and estimate their scores. Candidates can challenge the provisional answer key by paying a non-refundable fee of Rs 200 per question before the final answer key is prepared.

title: Exclusive | 'At 15, he should still be immature': ABD on protecting Sooryavanshi
content: Few cricketers have witnessed the evolution of modern cricket quite like AB de Villiers. From the rise of T20 leagues to the changing aspirations of young cricketers, the South African legend has lived through the sport's biggest transition.

title: Video: Shiv Sena neta assaults Dombivli hospital staff over treatment delay
content: Civic hospital staff in Kalyan-Dombivli suspended services protesting an assault by a corporator and relatives. The incident occurred after a perceived delay in treating a pregnant patient on Tuesday. Police filed an FIR against four men and one woman, including the corporator. Protesting staff vowed to continue their agitation until all accused are arrested. Private doctors threatened solidarity action if arrests were not made by Wednesday.

title: Rupee erases previous gains, tumbles 20 paise against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee opened weaker against the US dollar on Wednesday.  Rising crude oil prices and geopolitical events impacted currency markets.  The dollar index strengthened, and Brent crude futures climbed significantly.  Domestic stock markets also mirrored this cautious sentiment, opening lower.  Foreign institutional investors remained net buyers of equities on Tuesday.

title: US Navy's advanced warships plagued by fires and breakdowns, report claims
content: Recent incidents involving fires and electrical glitches on modern US Navy warships have sparked doubts regarding their dependability. Noteworthy cases like the USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Zumwalt reveal significant operational difficulties. Factors such as lengthy deployment periods and a heavy reliance on advanced technologies play a critical role in these challenges, compounded by workforce shortages and limited shipyard capabilities that threaten the Navy's readiness.

title: 11,12...14: Why do so many buildings not have a '13th floor'?
content: Many buildings omit the thirteenth floor due to buyer superstition and triskaidekaphobia. This practice is a global real estate phenomenon influencing design choices worldwide. Developers avoid the number to ensure faster sales and higher occupancy rates. From a practical standpoint, the thirteenth floor offers no structural or safety differences. Ultimately, buyer perception continues to shape real estate development decisions.

title: 'This is no country for women': Indian blogger reveals unseen Afghanistan
content: Indian travel creator Sharanya Iyer documented her solo journey through Taliban-ruled Afghanistan.  She explored dramatic mountains and met locals to understand their culture.  Her trip raised questions about whether tourism legitimizes the Taliban regime.  Iyer argued that boycotts hurt ordinary citizens and local economies.  She emphasized that responsible tourism can potentially aid citizens in difficult nations.

title: How 19-year-old Niharika became only Indian woman in Asian Games show jumping
content: At just 19, equestrian Niharika Singhania has earned a place in India's show jumping team for the 2026 Asian Games, becoming its only woman rider. After overcoming qualification hurdles and switching horses weeks before the deadline, she is now driven by an unwavering bond with her horses and ambitions that stretch beyond Aichi-Nagoya.

title: 'Boom': Trump after US strikes Iran, posts video of attacks
content: The United States conducted strikes against over eighty Iranian military targets. These actions were a direct response to Tehran's recent attacks on commercial shipping. Iran warned of necessary measures, raising concerns about the interim agreement's collapse. Bahrain sounded missile alert sirens following the American strikes on Iran. The attacks cast uncertainty over upcoming negotiations on a final agreement.

title: 'I felt I let the team down': Lionel Messi admits penalty miss left him devastated
content: Lionel Messi admitted feeling he let his team down after missing a crucial penalty. Argentina faced elimination trailing two goals before a stunning comeback unfolded. Cristian Romero, Messi, and Enzo Fernandez scored within thirteen minutes to secure victory. Messi's late equalizing goal provided immense relief and happiness for the squad. This remarkable fighting spirit propelled Argentina into the World Cup quarter-finals.

title: Why China's first submarine missile test in 43 years is ringing nuclear alarm bells
content: China launched a ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine into the Pacific Ocean on Monday, marking its first known submarine-launched ballistic missile test since 1982. The launch showcased the operational readiness of China's sea-based nuclear force, which forms one leg of the country's nuclear triad alongside land-based missiles and air-delivered nuclear weapons.

title: 12 years of civilisational diplomacy: How PM Modi restored India's shared heritage
content: One of the earliest initiatives came in Vietnam, where India signed a Memorandum of Understanding in 2014 to restore the UNESCO-listed Mỹ Sơn Sanctuary. Once the spiritual centre of the ancient Champa Kingdom, the Shaivite temple complex is regarded as one of Southeast Asia's most significant Hindu heritage sites. The Archaeological Survey of India led the conservation work under the bilateral agreement.

title: Explained: The VAR decision that left Egypt furious against Argentina
content: Argentina secured a dramatic 3-2 victory against Egypt in the World Cup Round of 16.  Egypt's camp expressed strong dissatisfaction with several officiating decisions during the match.  A disallowed Egyptian goal and the build-up to Argentina's winner fueled their anger.  Coach Hossam Hassan stated his team suffered clear injustice and unfair treatment.  Argentina advanced to the quarter-finals after this controversial and exciting encounter.

title: 'Life was enough': Student's final text to boyfriend before body found in Bengaluru lake
content: A 20-year-old undergraduate student was discovered deceased in Sankey Tank on Tuesday morning. Police suspect suicide after the student sent a message indicating intent to end her life. She reportedly entered the lake on Monday evening after leaving college and spending time nearby. Her body was found Tuesday morning after an extensive search by authorities and family. An investigation into the unnatural death is currently underway from all angles.

title: What did Hassan’s furious ‘X’ gesture mean? Egypt boss’ yellow card moment explained
content: Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan sparked controversy during Egypt’s dramatic 3-2 FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 defeat to Argentina after making an “X” gesture toward referee François Letexier. While the gesture is recognised by FIFA as a signal against racist abuse, its meaning in this context remains unclear. FIFA has not confirmed any investigation, though Hassan’s actions may face disciplinary review.

title: 'Tehran's actions unacceptable': US revokes license for Iranian oil sale
content: The United States revoked an Iran oil sanctions waiver after recent tanker attacks. Three tankers were struck by projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz. One vessel caught fire off the coast of Oman following the incident. The Iranian foreign ministry condemned the US move as a breach of commitment. These events threaten to disrupt vital shipping through the strategic waterway.

title: Annual pain: Season’s 1st heavy rain brings Delhi-NCR to its knees
content: Delhi received its most intense monsoon rains since July second on Tuesday. Waterlogging and uprooted trees caused significant traffic disruptions across the city. Flight operations were also impacted, with over 300 flights experiencing delays at IGI Airport. The minimum temperature rose to thirty degrees Celsius, marking the warmest July night in two years. Air quality deteriorated to moderate levels but is expected to improve soon.

title: Salman Khan's housing project in Goa under scanner; Bombay High Court issues notice
content: Salman Khan received a notice from the Goa High Court regarding alleged CRZ norm violations. A public interest litigation challenges construction at a Candolim property, citing environmental law breaches. The petition claims residential villas were built within a protected 'No Development Zone'. It also questions the validity of an occupancy certificate issued by the local Gram Panchayat. The court has sought responses from various government bodies and the actor.

title: FIFA launches racism probe after IShowSpeed abuse allegation during Argentina match
content: FIFA is investigating content creator IShowSpeed for alleged racist abuse.  This incident occurred during Argentina's World Cup match against Cape Verde.  Argentina secured a dramatic extra-time victory over Egypt in another thrilling game.  The defending champions came back from two goals down to win.  Argentina will now face Switzerland in the quarter-finals of the tournament.

title: Nolan Wells update: Family seeks answers after Horn Island trip
content: Nolan Xavier Wells, an 18-year-old Mississippi student-athlete, was found dead on Horn Island after going missing during a Fourth of July trip with friends. Authorities have not announced a cause of death and say the investigation is ongoing. His family has hired attorney Ben Crump and is calling for a thorough, transparent review of the circumstances surrounding his death.

title: Ira Khan’s video about her relationship resurfaces as Aamir Khan ties the knot
content: Ira Khan and Nupur Shikhare opened up about their journey together, touching on their experiences with depression and the challenges they've faced as a couple. They recounted their first argument and how they support each other through cyclical depressive episodes. Sharing stories of their initial meeting and growing affection, this candid conversation comes shortly after Ira's father, Aamir Khan, tied the knot again.

title: 'Dropped 35,000 feet in 2 minutes': Plane with 5 on board goes missing off Karachi
content: The missing aircraft, a Boeing 737 operated by Karachi-based K2 Airways, was flying from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates to Karachi when it reported a navigation system malfunction at around 9.18 pm local time, according to Pakistan's airport authority. Officials said radar data showed the aircraft suddenly descending and making a sharp change in direction at around 9.21 pm. Moments later, both radar and radio contact were lost about 155 nautical miles west of Karachi.

title: Lionel Messi, FIFA face bias claims after Argentina's win over Egypt
content: Argentina’s 3-2 victory over Egypt generated debate far beyond the final score. Lionel Messi’s missed penalty, controversial decisions and Gianni Infantino’s reaction from the stands became major talking points online. While some fans claimed Argentina received favorable treatment, no evidence supports those allegations. On the pitch, the defending champions recovered from adversity and earned another place in the World Cup quarter-finals.

title: Wayanad debris slip Live: Search on for 5 missing; Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan says detailed probe to be conducted
content: A day after a debris slip at the tunnel road construction site in Kerala's Wayanad district claimed three lives, extensive search operations are ongoing to find five people who are still missing

title: Why are Tamil Nadu and Kerala opposed to the proposed changes in Food Security law? | Explained
content: Read on to find out why ministers from Tamil Nadu and Kerala have voiced their concerns over the proposed amendments to the National Food Security Act

title: Tungabhadra dam gets new crest gates, but weak monsoon threatens paddy crop, survival of rice mills in Karnataka
content: A weak monsoon has left the Tungabhadra reservoir nearly empty, threatening not only this year’s paddy crop but also an entire economy built around premium Sona Masuri rice

title: Heavy rains bring relief to Delhi as Orange alerts issued across several States | Monsoon LIVE updates
content: Parts of southern Gujarat received “extremely heavy” rainfall, with the worst-affected being Surat district, where five persons died in rain-related incidents

title: Farmers urge govt. to clear power dues, activate Tunga Lift Irrigation Project
content: The project benefits 7,500 acres of agricultural land across 28 villages; however, it has remained defunct as power supply was disconnected due to non-payment of electricity bills

title: Avoid unnecessary inspections, says CMO after backlash to video posted by Minister Keerthana
content: Minister for Law R. Nirmalkumar made the statement after the video of an inspection done by Industries Minister S. Keerthana at a government school in Sivakasi came in for severe criticism

title: Bleak scenario: Karnataka’s hydel storage second-lowest in last 10 years
content: The cumulative storage in all the three hydel reservoirs account for a mere 14.9% of the full capacity on July 3, 2026, as against 44.05% during corresponding period last year

title: Baruipur rape-murder case: One key accused killed in police encounter
content: Violence had erupted on July 5, when the body of the minor was recovered from a pond at Surjapur in Baruipur; it was the first police encounter since BJP came to power in West Bengal

title: Madhya Pradesh woman delivers four babies in autorickshaw, all newborns die; relatives blame lack of ambulance
content: Her husband claims that if an ambulance had been available on time, his children could have been saved

title: Cauvery basin farmers abandon or delay sowing amid water crisis
content: While reservoir levels are worryingly low, lakes, ponds, and small water bodies are also running out of water in the Cauvery basin region

title: Prepare for possible drought, drinking water crisis, Minister tells officials
content: Koppal records 31% rainfall deficit; district asked to draw contingency plans, create rural jobs, ensure crop insurance coverage, and streamline Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls

title: Mavigun more accessible, cost-effective than Amaravati, says Peddireddi Ramachandra Reddy
content: The former Minister said the three-city region of Machilipatnam, Vijayawada and Guntur, proposed by Y.S. Jagan Mohan Reddy, would spread development to backward areas of Krishna and Guntur

title: Red gram farmers watch saplings wilt, hope wither as Karnataka awaits rains
content: Unsown red gram fields in Kalaburagi are wilting, while sown cotton fields in Raichur and Yadgir are bearing the brunt of a deficit monsoon, with the next fortnight likely to determine the fate of the kharif season

title: Heavy rain lashes Kodagu; holiday declared for schools, colleges on July 8
content: Bhagamandala records rainfall of 165 mm in 24 hours; tree fall damages house, road caves in after overnight downpour

title: PM Modi, President Subianto visit Prambanan Temple in Indonesia
content: India and Indonesia, a day ago, exchanged a Letter of Intent to start a project on the conservation and restoration of the temple complex with assistance from India

title: A missing girl, mob violence, and an encounter: What happened in West Bengal’s Baruipur?
content: The rape and murder and subsequent mob violence had posed a challenge to the newly-inducted BJP government in West Bengal

title: Floods, landslides affect several Arunachal districts
content: According to the SEOC, Upper Siang was the worst-hit district, followed by Siang and Kra-Daadi; Upper Siang recorded landslides at 16 locations

title: Temperature levels high as monsoon remains subdued in Tamil Nadu
content: Chennai has been experiencing elevated day temperatures and hot afternoons since the start of the week

title: APCRDA approves better benefits for Amaravati land pooling farmers
content: The Chief Minister directed officials to ensure that each of the 25 townships proposed across the 217 sq. km Amaravati capital region includes at least one national or international school and a modern hospital

title: ED raids in U.P. against former SP MLA
content: Multiple cases booked against the politician include those related to cheating, forgery, extortion, attempt to murder, dacoity etc., registered under the IPC, UP Gangsters Act, Goondas Act etc., says officials

title: Prashant Kishor calls upcoming by-election at Bankipur Assembly a “referendum” on BJP government in Bihar
content: The by-poll at Bankipur constituency is scheduled to be held on July 30, and the results will come out on August 3. The Bankipur Assembly seat had fallen vacant after BJP’s Nitin Nabin, who had won the poll in the 2025 Assembly election, was later elected as party national president and then the Rajya Sabha member

title: Significance of Guwahati Declaration adopted by BRICS nations | Explained
content: The Guwahati Declaration underscores the need to bolster the timely exchange of information and best practices, consistent with national laws and international obligations

title: India supports two-state solution for solving Israel-Palestine conflict, says PM Modi
content: In a press statement in Jakarta while presiding over the signing of multiple agreements including the delivery of BrahMos supersonic cruise missile system and Astra Mk-1 beyond the visual-range air-to-air missiles to Indonesia, Mr. Modi says India believes that the role of dialogue and diplomacy has become more important than ever before

title: Police constable shoots himself with service rifle inside patrol van in Kalaburagi
content: A 30-year-old City Armed Reserve (CAR) police constable allegedly shot himself with a service rifle inside a stationary police patrolling vehicle at the busy Jagat Circle in Kalaburagi city on Tuesday night.

title: ED raids five locations to probe Trinamool bank accounts, funds for chartered flights
content: “Searches are being conducted at five premises in the matter of alleged dishonest financial dealings, unlawful collection of money and routing of suspected funds through certain bank accounts of AITC,” says sources

title: Drass witnesses Shauryathon, donation of CT scan machine by Shiv Sena
content: Shiv Sena had donated a CT scan machine and an ambulance to the hospital, says Shiv Sena MP Shrikant Shinde

title: Wayanad debris slip: CM Satheesan holds emergency meeting; Agriculture Minister terms it man-made disaster
content: Agriculture Minister T. Siddique said the accident was caused by unscientifically stacked excavated soil, and alleged that the contractor’s failure to remove the accumulated soil on time led to the incident

title: After pro-separatist book fiasco, J&K L-G calls for examination of websites of J&K varsities, colleges for anti-India content
content: The L-G’s move came days after a book, titled ‘Great Personalities and Legends of J&K’, profiled Kashmir separatist leaders

title: Traffic bottleneck outside Sarath City mall leaves motorists stranded for hours on Gachibowli–Miyapur corridor; underpass, skywalk pitched
content: With up to 2.5 lakh weekend visitors, the mall frontage has become one of Hyderabad’s worst traffic hotspots as vehicles on six-lane squeeze into two-lane road. Inadequate pick-up and drop-off space cripple movement.

title: NCERT publishes revised Class 8 Social Science textbook, rewrites controversial chapter on judiciary
content: The controversial portions along with references to judicial backlog and two major court verdicts have been dropped; the revised textbook states in its acknowledgements that it has been published pursuant to the review process undertaken by complying with the Supreme Court’s directions

title: Baruipur rape-murder case: West Bengal Police intensify probe; examine shack as possible crime scene
content: “Three arrested accused were being questioned even as investigators were awaiting the forensic and viscera examination reports,” says senior police officer

title: Record 1.3 lakh pilgrims visit Amarnath shrine in 5 days in Kashmir
content: Our goal is to make the pilgrimage a truly memorable experience for everyone and they have access to the best possible amenities, says Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha

title: Ahead of Delhi protest, NC leaders meet civil society, pass resolution on Statehood for J&K
content: National Conference leaders term the delay in restoration of Statehood as ‘inordinate’ and describe the Centre’s ‘appropriate time phrase’ as a ‘vague and timeless expression’

title: Chief Minister Vijay “blackmailed” children by appealing to them, two election petitioners allege before Madras High Court
content: Justice V. Lakshminarayanan orders notices on the election petitions filed by DMK’s R.D. Shekar and S. Inigo Irudayaraj who had lost in Perambur and Tiruchi East constituencies

title: Staff stole cash, security protocols not enforced: Ram Temple SIT report
content: CCTV footage shows some counting staff hiding currency notes in clothes, pockets, and shoes; SIT identified procedural flaws in management of donations, to conduct re-audit of accounts

title: Decision over alliance with TVK yet to be reached, says Thirumavalavan
content: On July 1, the Chief Minister spoke to the parties that extended support, but no final decision was taken, he clarifies

title: Arcot MLA clarifies his absence at AIADMK consultative meeting
content: The Arcot MLA said that he had informed the party leadership about his inability to attend the consultative meeting at the AIADMK’s headquarters in Chennai

title: Cracks wide open in Punjab Congress as Channi camp skips Baghel’s outreach meetings
content: Punjab in-charge meets District Congress Committee chiefs, senior leaders as part of preparations for Assembly election; Warring downplays crisis, says Channi to meet Baghel within a day or two

title: 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts: High Court upholds death penalty for 38; government calls verdict a historic victory
content: On July 26, 2008, a series of 21 bomb blasts ripped through Ahmedabad in a span of 70 minutes, killing 56 persons and injuring 200 others

title: Residents oppose road cutting to lay new water pipelines in Tirupattur
content: Work to lay new water pipelines was proposed at a cost of ₹4.75 crore by the civic body a few months ago

title: Gang held for possession of ganja near Katpadi
content: The police said that as part of surveillance, they were patrolling the check post near Katpadi when the team intercepted the gang

title: Bengaluru firm builds a platform on which users can build their family trees up to eight generations; digitise family heirlooms
content: Its existing algorithms have the capacity to trace and store ancestral roots seven or up to eight generations

content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more . July 7, 2026 e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective .

title: Decoding Op Hard Ball: Inside US Probe In Lawrence Bishnoi Gang, Its Rivals
content: US investigators claimed Bishnoi projected himself as a "patriot," "nationalist," and "deeply religious" individual through social media and media interviews.

title: Meghalaya Urges Centre To Ease Coal Mining Approvals For Tribal Landowners
content: The move would enable small tribal coal holders to obtain lawful mineral concessions and the required approvals within Meghalaya instead of having to seek clearances from central agencies.

title: Key Accused In Rape-Murder Of 11-Year-Old In Bengal Killed In Encounter
content: Pravash Mondal was shot when he tried to snatch a policeman's firearm and flee during the reconstruction of the crime, sources told NDTV.

title: US Court Relief For Indian-Origin Doctor Who Drove Family In Tesla Off Cliff
content: On January 2, 2023, Patel was on a road trip with his wife and two children when he drove his white Tesla off a cliff in Northern California.

title: Cities Are Turning Into Heat Traps. Climate Change Isn't The Only Reason
content: The "Urban Heat Island" effect -- an invisible phenomenon that is quietly making India's cities several degrees hotter than their surroundings.

title: China Weighs Curbs On Overseas Access To Advanced AI Models: Report
content: Beijing's potential restrictions on frontier AI models threaten global tech markets with higher costs while treating advanced software as a critical asset.
